Reason_Present: Fumigation treatment used to reduce microbial contamination in spices
Health_Concern: Carcinogenic chemical; long-term exposure associated with cancer risk
Country_Agency: Hong Kong – Centre for Food Safety (CFS)
Limit_or_Threshold_Cited: Hong Kong permits only residues within legal MRLs; regulator reported levels exceeding the legal limit. US EPA tolerance: 7 ppm for dried spices/herbs. EU: generally extremely low default MRL (often 0.01 mg/kg). UK: EU-aligned MRL approach, enhanced monitoring.
Regulatory_Action_and_Date: Sales suspended; consumers instructed not to consume (Apr 2024)
Current_Status: Under heightened international scrutiny; no global blanket ban

Limit_or_Threshold_Cited: Above legal limit. US: commodity-specific tolerance. EU: pesticide residue limits apply. UK: enhanced import checks.
Above legal limit. US: commodity-specific tolerance. EU: MRL approach. UK: monitoring.

Reason_Present: Used as sterilisation fumigant during spice processing
Limit_or_Threshold_Cited: Above legal limit. US: 0.01 mg/kg. EU: very low residue limits. UK: enhanced import controls.

Reason_Present: Used as sterilisation fumigant during spice processing
Limit_or_Threshold_Cited: Food considered not compliant due to ETO exceeding Singapore's regulatory requirement

Reason_Present: Raw spice contamination from soil, water, handling, storage
Health_Concern: Food poisoning, fever, diarrhea; severe risk for vulnerable groups
Limit_or_Threshold_Cited: Zero tolerance for Salmonella in ready-to-eat foods. EU: not permitted in ready-to-eat foods. UK: zero tolerance.
Regulatory_Action_and_Date: Voluntary recall (June 2023); FDA public health alert; Import Alert 99-19
Current_Status: Recalled; remaining inventory destroyed; import detention for affected products

Limit_or_Threshold_Cited: Zero tolerance. EU: not permitted. UK: zero tolerance.

Reason_Present: Illegal colourant historically used in chilli/spice products
Country_Agency: No confirmed major international recall identified
Limit_or_Threshold_Cited: No major documented international recall specifically involving leading Indian packaged spice brands was identified; Sudan dye alerts have historically involved chilli products from multiple countries.
Current_Status: No confirmed major recall for these brands
